text: A 70% reduction in training volume over 3 weeks maintains 5-km race performance in well-conditioned distance runners.
Reducing training frequency by only 17% (from 6 to 5 days/week) while cutting volume by 70% preserves endurance performance during a taper.
VO2max is maintained after 3 weeks of 70% volume reduction in trained runners, indicating aerobic capacity is resilient to short-term training reductions.
Time to exhaustion during VO2max testing increased by 9.5% after 3 weeks of reduced training, suggesting improved fatigue resistance despite lower volume.
Maintaining high-intensity training (~95% VO2max intervals and races) during a volume taper is associated with preserved race performance.
Estimated plasma volume decreases by approximately 5.6% after 3 weeks of reduced training, which may contribute to a small increase in maximal heart rate.
Maximal heart rate increases by approximately 4 bpm after 3 weeks of 70% volume reduction, possibly due to plasma volume contraction.
